recommended_next_target.name: Fixed safety/filter classifier submodels
recommended_next_target.baseline_comparison: Programmable accelerators are strong, but isolation and fallback make fixed or semi-fixed safety filters testable.
recommended_next_target.rationale: Small repeated classifiers can be long-lived, isolated, and tolerant of conservative fallback.
recommended_next_target.falsifier: Demote if policy churn forces weekly updates or false-positive/negative cost requires full programmable execution.

top_ranked.name: Fixed safety/filter classifier submodels
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: Programmable accelerators are strong, but isolation and fallback make fixed or semi-fixed safety filters testable.
top_ranked.rationale: Small repeated classifiers can be long-lived, isolated, and tolerant of conservative fallback.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if policy churn forces weekly updates or false-positive/negative cost requires full programmable execution.

top_ranked.name: Small always-on wake-word or router models
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: Programmable accelerator cost may dominate for always-on tiny models; fixed local logic has a plausible idle-energy niche.
top_ranked.rationale: Always-on routing has stable weights, simple interfaces, and high aggregate reuse despite small per-request work.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if the router must track fast-changing model menus or if CPU/NPU idle states already match the energy target.

top_ranked.name: Repeated retrieval/reranking feature transforms
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: Software caching helps, but repeated feature transforms can remain hot across requests.
top_ranked.rationale: Reranking pipelines often reuse stable transforms at high volume and can fall back to programmable paths.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if workload-specific feature changes dominate or batching/caching erases memory movement.

top_ranked.name: Mixture-of-experts router or dispatch logic
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: Software runtimes already optimize dispatch, so credit only the stable high-volume router core.
top_ranked.rationale: Routers are reused heavily and smaller than experts, but integration with dynamic serving is nontrivial.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if expert churn or load balancing changes the router distribution faster than quarterly.

top_ranked.name: Positional encoding / RoPE / deterministic transform support
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: Deterministic transforms are easy to optimize in software or programmable logic; physicalized weights add little.
top_ranked.rationale: This is stable and reusable, but it is not obviously weight-dominated.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if profiling shows negligible memory/energy share versus attention and KV movement.

top_ranked.name: Domain-specific adapter or LoRA blocks with slow update cadence
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: The software-optimized baseline is dangerous here because adapters are already compact and easy to swap.
top_ranked.rationale: Some long-lived enterprise adapters have enough stability to justify semi-fixed treatment, not permanent masks.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if tenant-specific updates are monthly or if memory savings from adapter caching reaches 50%.

top_ranked.name: Fixed or slowly changing embedding tables for constrained vocabularies/domains
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: Programmable memory hierarchy improvements compete directly; only constrained stable vocabularies survive.
top_ranked.rationale: Constrained domains can make table storage stable and reusable, but vocabulary churn is a major risk.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if vocabulary/logit head variants churn or if compression/caching removes the movement bottleneck.

top_ranked.name: Quantization/dequantization and scaling constants
top_ranked.baseline_comparison: Quantization is already a software/compiler strength, so fixed constants alone get limited credit.
top_ranked.rationale: Constants are stable and easy to integrate, but energy upside may be too small for a dedicated substrate.
top_ranked.falsifier: Demote if compiler fusion removes standalone movement or if formats change per model family.

anti_targets.name: Full frontier LLM dense weights burned permanently into fixed logic
anti_targets.baseline_comparison: Even if per-request energy looks attractive, update cadence, yield, stranded capital, and programmable baselines dominate.
anti_targets.rationale: Superficially large energy upside fails the null because the entire model is high-churn and hard to repair.
anti_targets.falsifier: Promote only with evidence of multi-year frozen weights, extreme volume, repairability, and no competitive programmable path.

anti_targets.name: Frequently updated tenant-specific fine-tunes or adapters
anti_targets.baseline_comparison: Software adapter loading and routing are designed for this case; fixed substrates strand value quickly.
anti_targets.rationale: Update churn prevents amortization even if adapters are small.
anti_targets.falsifier: Promote only if a tenant model freezes for quarters and reaches high shared reuse.

anti_targets.name: High-churn vocabulary/logit-head variants
anti_targets.baseline_comparison: Tokenizer/head churn is exactly where software indirection and table updates are cheap.
anti_targets.rationale: Variants look table-like but update and SKU fragmentation erase the fixed-weight case.
anti_targets.falsifier: Promote only for a constrained vocabulary with stable deployment and high shared traffic.

anti_targets.name: Dynamic attention over live context as fixed physical logic
anti_targets.baseline_comparison: KV/cache software and programmable accelerators are the right baseline; live context is not static weight state.
anti_targets.rationale: Attention dataflow is important but too dynamic to burn as fixed weights.
anti_targets.falsifier: Promote only for a narrow fixed transform inside attention with measured stability and fallback.

anti_targets.name: Training/optimizer state and gradient computation
anti_targets.baseline_comparison: This is not inference weight reuse; programmable training systems remain the relevant substrate.
anti_targets.rationale: Optimizer state mutates continuously and cannot amortize fixed physicalization.
anti_targets.falsifier: Promote only if the scope changes to inference-only frozen auxiliary weights.

method_notes: scores are modeled/inferred/speculative heuristics, not measured silicon results
energy_upside_vs_baseline and software_baseline_resistance are reduced as software_memory_savings increases
zero reuse volume or near-zero update interval caps viability because fixed costs cannot amortize
baseline calibration comes from winner shares in breakeven_summary.json and grid ranges in breakeven_grid.csv
